semantic.role: Language Pattern Definition for Code Analysis
responsibilities: Defining export patterns for classes (class $NAME, public class $NAME) and interfaces (interface $NAME, public interface $NAME)
Defining usage patterns for method calls ($OBJ.$NAME($$ARGS)) and object instantiation (new $NAME($$ARGS))
outOfScope: Other language constructs (e.g., annotations, enums, generics)

English.purpose: This document provides a set of pattern definitions used by the ArchSpine system to recognize key structural elements in Java-like source code. It enables automatic identification of imports, class/interface declarations, method calls, and object creations, which are essential for code mirroring and dependency analysis.
English.context_and_audience: The intended audience is developers who maintain or extend the language support layer of ArchSpine. It describes how the system matches code constructs using placeholder-based patterns, making it easy to add or modify recognition rules for different programming languages.
key_takeaways: Patterns are defined under three categories: imports, exports, and usages.
Export patterns recognize both default and public class/interface declarations, each with a unique ID and kind.
Usage patterns cover method invocations and object instantiation, using placeholders like $OBJ, $NAME, and $ARGS.
The patterns use a simple, template‑like syntax with placeholders beginning with '$'.
